title: National Occupation List occupations used for an AEWV :: Immigration New Zealand
content:
## National Occupation List occupations used for an AEWV
These National Occupation List (NOL) occupations can be used for an Accredited Employer Work Visa (AEWV)
On 9 March 2026, 47 more NOL occupations at skill levels 1 to 3 were added to the list of NOL occupations we recognise for an AEWV.
New occupations recognised under the National Occupation List and annual median wage increase
The National Occupation List (NOL) groups jobs together into 'occupations' that have similar tasks and responsibilities. NOL skill levels range from 1 to 5, with 1 being the most skilled and 5 being less skilled.
The NOL will replace the Australian and New Zealand Standard Classification of Occupations (ANZSCO). As we transition to the NOL, we will update these lists as we recognise more NOL occupations.
Use the NOL occupation code if your job is on one of these lists and you are applying for:
- an Accredited Employer Work Visa (AEWV), or
- a job check for an AEWV.

## How to find your NOL occupation and skill level
To use a NOL occupation you must meet the requirements of that occupation. You can use the Aria tool, or National Occupation List on the Statistics New Zealand website to find the requirements for a NOL occupation.

### Searching the NOL (Aria tool) for occupation requirements
Once you have found the occupation or NOL code on this page, you can then search the tool for the code. The requirements are described in the 'Definition' tab when you click on the occupation.
National Occupation List V3.0.0 — Statistics New Zealand
Some NOL occupations are not recognised for an AEWV. If your NOL occupation is not listed in tables 1 to 6 on this page, you cannot apply for a job check or AEWV using it. You must use the relevant ANZSCO code instead.
Find your job's skill level

### Applying for a job check
If you are applying for a job check, make sure the NOL code you use is accurate by checking the:
- responsibilities of the occupation, and
- qualifications or work experience required.
We will not give you more time to advertise a role or engage with Work and Income if you apply for a job check for an NOL level 1, 2 or 3 role, but we assess it as being level 4 or 5. We do not currently recognise any NOL skill level 4 or 5 jobs — if we assess it as a lower level you need to apply again using an ANZSCO code.
